description: Mirror blind-spot for lateral crossing: on a Normal Road, a truck enters from the ego's right/front-right on a constructed collision course while the nominal left escape corridor contains a pedestrian and is partially blocked; a close follower makes rear-end risk relevant. Truck lateral start is fixed and its lateral speed is derived from the swept TTC.
hypothesis: For right-side crossing threats with the left escape side occupied/blocked, the dominant maneuver should switch from nominal left-drift to hard braking or right/hold-lane avoidance as TTC shrinks and the left escape gap falls below a clearance threshold.
